annotations.text: The olfactory hallucinations suggest a focal seizure arising in the uncus.
The mass in the brain may be neoplastic or nonneoplastic in origin.
Seizures develop in about 50% of patients with temporal-lobe tumors, and approximately half of these patients have complex partial seizures.
The first possibility is unlikely on the basis of clinical and imaging findings, and the description of the mass does not support a diagnosis of abscess.
The caries are most likely incidental.

Sjögren’s syndrome may be primary or secondary to another connective-tissue disease, such as systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, or scleroderma, and neurologic involvement is present in approximately 20% of affected patients.

Tumefactive, enhancing nonneoplastic brain lesions can be caused by inflammatory diseases, such as multiple sclerosis, acute disseminated encephalomyelitis, sarcoidosis, Behçet’s disease with neurologic involvement, tuberculosis, fungal infections, and syphilitic gumma.
the mass is associated with noncontiguous meningeal disease, which suggests a diagnosis of a granuloma.
Given the presence of calcification and the prepontine lesion, which suggests meningeal involvement, a unifying diagnosis could be cerebral sarcoidosis.
High-resolution CT of the chest was performed after the administration of contrast material.
The patient’s serum angiotensin-converting–enzyme level was normal.
The hypothalamus and the pituitary are the most common sites of involvement, although sarcoidosis can involve any region of the brain.
this constellation of events is suggestive of a generalized seizure.
This finding will most likely prompt an adjustment of the patient’s immunosuppressive regimen, possibly with the introduction of infliximab.
if there is prominent hilar lymphadenopathy, it is likely to be seen on the chest radiograph.
In this case, high-resolution CT was needed to identify the subtle hilar lymphadenopathy, and even then, no abnormalities were identified in the lung parenchyma.
I think that we would have been obligated to confirm the diagnosis with a biopsy either way.
